mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, or signal accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals immediate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO increases during recovery phases, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% while the unemployment rate remains below 4.5%, monetary tightening outpaces labor market slack, triggering margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% and UNRATE remains below 4.5%
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with > 55% accuracy during tightening cycles, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized indicates a severe demand contraction that forces corporate earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of low-GDP-growth quarters, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decrease in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) during recovery phases, or shows negative predictive accuracy.
